definitions: to do or say something again
to change or improve something, like writing
to fix something that is broken
to try not to do something or to push against something
to stay the same or to stay in one place
to make something again or to do something for fun
to respond or act because something happened
to speak to someone in a strong way because they did something wrong

questions: The teacher places special EMPHASIS on using complete sentences in our writing.
Please EMPHASIZE the most important facts in your presentation.
The coach EMPHASIZED the importance of teamwork before the game.
The poster was EMPHASIZING the need to recycle and protect the environment.
Her EMPHATIC answer made it clear that she strongly disagreed.
He EMPHATICALLY reminded the class to study for the test.

lesson-input-data.paragraph: Before our class art show, we needed to ____________ (put in order) our supplies and ____________ (choose first) which projects to finish first. I wanted to ____________ (add style) my poster by adding colorful borders and creative lettering. Our teacher reminded us to ____________ (stress importance) the main message of our work and think about what our designs might ____________ (stand for). As we worked together, we tried to ____________ (work together) our ideas so everyone felt included. In the end, we were able to ____________ (make better) our time and create a display we were proud of.

questions: Wearing sunscreen can help __________ the risk of getting a painful sunburn.
The dove is often used to __________ peace and hope.
We worked together to __________ the animals based on their habitats.
After reading the article, we were asked to __________ the main ideas in one paragraph.
The students decided to __________ the story by acting out their favorite scenes.
Technology contiues to __________ communication by connecting people around the world instantly.
The library plans to __________ its computer systems to better serve students and teachers.
Before beginning the experiment, we were asked to __________ what might happen.

definitions: to stand for or represent something else
to make a smart guess based on what you already know
to make something more exciting or emotional, often by acting it out or adding extra details
to help pay for something so it costs less
to design or change something so it has a special look
to make use of something for a purpose
to give special attention to something important
to think deeply about big ideas, such as life, truth, or what is right

constructorRows.left: in + stite + ute + ion + al + ize

lesson-input-data.paragraph:
1.  Our reading __________ improved after we practiced every day.
2.  We made __________ progress as we learned new math skills.
3.  I hope to __________ from fourth grade feeling proud of my hard    work.
4.  Our class made a lot of __________ on our science projects this          week.
5.  I can __________ my writing by adding better details.
6.  Missing homework can __________ your final score.
7.  The dog became __________ when another dog took its toy.
8.  __________ meets to make laws for our country.

lesson-activities.lesson-name: PER- Fill in the Morph paragraph

lesson-input-data.paragraph: With ____________ from her teacher, Rana worked hard to ____________ her classmates to ____________ a short play at the school assembly. Even when some students wanted to give up, she encouraged them to ____________ and keep practicing. As they worked, excitement seemed to ____________ the classroom, and a feeling of teamwork began to ____________ the group. By the end, everyone was proud of what they had accomplished together.

1.  The teacher will ____________ the class during the test.
2.  The king chose his son as his ____________.
3.  I went to the ____________ when I felt sick.
4.  The ____________ showed us how the machine works.
5.  Our town is in the farming ____________.
6.  The company will ____________ our school event.
7.  Time is an important ____________ in solving the    problem.
8.  The smoke ____________ beeped when the battery was low.
